text: Simon's deepest contribution was not a warning but a reorientation: he asked us to stop measuring society's health by how much information it produces and start measuring it by how wisely it allocates attention. A civilization that generates brilliant science, literature, and journalism — but whose citizens have no attention left to receive it — has not solved its knowledge problem. Tim Wu, in 'The Attention Merchants,' later extended this by showing that attentional allocation is not neutral: it is contested, commercially engineered, and carries real costs for self-determination. Simon gave us the diagnosis; the question of governance remains open.
question: According to the passage, what measure of civilizational health did Simon propose replacing information production with?

options.text: The quality of scientific publications per capita
The commercial value generated by media platforms
The wisdom with which a society allocates its attention
The percentage of citizens who consume news daily
